title: Gymnogramma nivea
taxonomy: Gymnogramma nivea is a species of plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Gymnogramma, which is a group of plants within the dandelion-like plants. The species nivea is characterized by its white or grayish color and its unique growth habits. Gymnogramma nivea is often classified as a type of thistle, although its exact classification may vary depending on the source. This plant is native to certain parts of the world and is often found in dry, rocky areas.

intro: Gymnogramma nivea, also known as White Gymnogramma, is a plant species belonging to the family Compositae (Asteraceae), characterized by its white, powdery hairs and its ability to thrive in a wide range of habitats. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Gymnogramma nivea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Gymnogramma nivea is typically prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion, and is also used in the form of tincture and powder to treat various health conditions.
